STAKEHOLDER NOTE: this recipe calls for Custer Feldspar, which is discontinued (extinct as of 2024) and cannot be purchased new. Jake's Glazy recipe is locked and can't be edited to reflect the substitute. Cost here is computed using the G-200 (EU) Potassium Feldspar substitute -- see that material's notes in db/glazes.db. Chemistry isn't identical (lower iron, higher purity), so this recipe may need reformulation to hit its original fired result; this cost analysis doesn't address that.

STAKEHOLDER NOTE (affects how to read $/lb): applied as a very thin coat -- just enough Ball Clay to make the Black Copper Oxide stick. The high copper density/availability at the surface is what produces the dramatic flame-reduction effects; that same density is what makes it one of the most expensive recipes here per pound. Per Jake, 2026-07-04: this $/lb figure overstates real per-piece cost since so little material is used per application -- do not compare this recipe to others on $/lb alone without accounting for coat thickness.
